stimulus: Most of the exoplanets confirmed by transit photometry orbit relatively close to their host stars, with orbital periods of only a few days. In 2018, ______blank researchers confirmed a planet orbiting Kepler-1654, a Sun-like star, with an orbital period stretching beyond two Earth years. Called Kepler-1654 b, it is one of the longest-period planets confirmed by the Kepler mission.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "By contrast" logically signals that the information about Kepler-1654 b—that it orbits its star with a period of more than two Earth years—contrasts with the previous information about most confirmed exoplanets, which orbit close to their host stars with periods of only a few days.

Choice B is incorrect because "likewise" illogically signals that Kepler-1654 b's orbit is similar to those of most exoplanets, when it actually represents a striking contrast. Choice C is incorrect because "therefore" illogically signals that the discovery is a result of the previous information, when it actually presents a contrasting case. Choice D is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that Kepler-1654 b exemplifies the typical short-period exoplanet pattern, when it actually contrasts with it.
